prompt.text: Du gibst wieder, was jemand gesagt hat — MIT einer Person danach (… me). Lösungswort aus t-o-l-d.

items.hintDe: Es ist die Vergangenheitsform von tell und braucht immer eine Person danach.
items.explainDe: told = sagte (jemandem). Du brauchst es immer mit einer Person: told me, told us, told Sully.

prompt.text: Du gibst wieder, was jemand gesagt hat — OHNE Person danach (… that …). Lösungswort aus s-a-i-d.

items.hintDe: Es ist die Vergangenheitsform von say und steht ohne Person: said that ...
items.explainDe: said = sagte. Es steht ohne Person danach: He said that ... (nie "said me").

prompt.text: He said that he likes music.

answers.text: He said that he liked music.

items.hintDe: Nach said muss das Verb eine Zeit zurück: aus likes wird liked.
items.explainDe: Das einleitende said steht in der Vergangenheit, also auch das Verb danach: likes → liked.

prompt.text: She said me that she was hungry.

answers.text: She told me that she was hungry.
She told me she was hungry.
She said that she was hungry.

items.hintDe: Direkt vor der Person (me) darf nicht said stehen.
items.explainDe: Mit Person danach brauchst du told: She told me ... "said me" gibt es nicht.

prompt.text: She told that she was going home.

answers.text: She said that she was going home.
She told me that she was going home.
She told us that she was going home.

items.hintDe: told braucht eine Person danach. Entweder du fügst eine Person ein (told me) oder du nimmst said.
items.explainDe: "told that" ohne Person ist falsch. Richtig: said that ... oder told me/us that ...

prompt.text: He said he can't come to the concert.

answers.text: He said he couldn't come to the concert.
He said he could not come to the concert.
He said that he couldn't come to the concert.
He said that he could not come to the concert.

items.hintDe: can't muss eine Zeit zurück zu couldn't.
items.explainDe: Nach said geht can't zurück zu couldn't. Also: He said he couldn't come to the concert.

prompt.text: He said he will visit us the day after.

answers.text: He said he would visit us the day after.
He said that he would visit us the day after.

items.hintDe: will muss eine Zeit zurück zu would.
items.explainDe: Nach said wird will zu would. Also: He said he would visit us the day after.

prompt.text: She said she is reading a book.

answers.text: She said she was reading a book.
She said that she was reading a book.

items.hintDe: is muss eine Zeit zurück zu was.
items.explainDe: is + -ing wird beim Wiedergeben zu was + -ing. Also: She said she was reading a book.

prompt.text: Harten said Sully that runway 1 was free.

answers.text: Harten told Sully that runway 1 was free.
Harten said to Sully that runway 1 was free.

items.hintDe: Direkt vor der Person (Sully) steht told, nicht said.
items.explainDe: Mit Person danach: told Sully. Oder said to Sully. "said Sully" allein ist falsch. (So steht es in der Story.)

prompt.text: They said they are waiting for the train.

answers.text: They said they were waiting for the train.
They said that they were waiting for the train.

items.hintDe: Bei they verschiebt sich are zu were.
items.explainDe: are + -ing wird zu were + -ing. Also: They said they were waiting for the train.

prompt.text: "I am tired." — He said that he ___ tired.

items.hintDe: Das einleitende Verb (said) steht in der Vergangenheit, also schiebst du auch im Nebensatz eine Zeit zurück: am/is wird zu was.
items.explainDe: Wenn du wiedergibst, was jemand gesagt hat, und das einleitende Verb in der Vergangenheit steht, geht die Zeit eine Stufe zurück. Aus am/is wird was.

prompt.text: "I am hungry." — She said that she ___ hungry.

items.hintDe: am/is verschiebt sich eine Zeit zurück zu was.
items.explainDe: Nach said geht die Zeit zurück: aus am/is wird was. Also: She said that she was hungry.

prompt.text: "I am busy." — He said that he ___ busy.

items.hintDe: am verschiebt sich eine Zeit zurück zu was.
items.explainDe: Aus am wird beim Wiedergeben was. Also: He said that he was busy.

prompt.text: "I like pizza, but I don't eat it very often." — He said that he ___ pizza, but he ___ it very often.

items.hintDe: Beide Verben gehen eine Zeit zurück: like wird zur 2. Form, und das verneinte don't wird zu didn't.
items.explainDe: Nach said schiebst du beide Verben zurück: like → liked, don't eat → didn't eat.

prompt.text: "I am reading a great book." — She said that she ___ reading a great book.

items.hintDe: Das am vor reading verschiebt sich zu was (verlaufende Handlung in der Vergangenheit).
items.explainDe: am/is + -ing wird beim Wiedergeben zu was + -ing. Also: She said that she was reading a great book.

prompt.text: "We don't want to leave." — They said that they ___ want to leave.

items.hintDe: Die Verneinung don't geht eine Zeit zurück zu didn't.
items.explainDe: Aus don't wird beim Wiedergeben didn't. Also: They said that they didn't want to leave.

prompt.text: "We can't make the runway." — Sully said that they ___ make the runway.

items.hintDe: can't verschiebt sich eine Zeit zurück zu couldn't.
items.explainDe: Aus can/can't wird beim Wiedergeben could/couldn't. Also: Sully said that they couldn't make the runway.

prompt.text: "We will land on the river." — Sully said that they ___ land on the river.

items.hintDe: will verschiebt sich eine Zeit zurück zu would.
items.explainDe: Aus will wird beim Wiedergeben would. Also: Sully said that they would land on the river.

prompt.text: "I must land the plane on the river." — Sully said that he ___ ___ land the plane on the river.

items.hintDe: must verschiebt sich beim Wiedergeben zu had to (zwei Wörter).
items.explainDe: Aus must wird had to. Also: Sully said that he had to land the plane on the river.

prompt.text: "I can swim very well." — She said that she ___ swim very well.

items.hintDe: can verschiebt sich eine Zeit zurück zu could.
items.explainDe: Aus can wird beim Wiedergeben could. Also: She said that she could swim very well.

prompt.text: "I saw a good film yesterday." — He ___ me that he ___ a good film the day before.

items.hintDe: Vor me brauchst du told. Das Verb saw (2. Form) geht noch eine Stufe zurück zur Form had + 3. Form.
items.explainDe: Mit Person (me) nimmst du told. Past simple (saw) wird beim Wiedergeben zu had + 3. Form: had seen.

prompt.text: "We can come to your party tomorrow." — They said that they ___ come to ___ party the day after.

items.hintDe: can wird zu could. Und das Pronomen wechselt die Perspektive: "your" (deine) wird aus meiner Sicht zu "my".
items.explainDe: can → could, und das Personenwort passt sich an: aus your wird my. Also: They said that they could come to my party the day after.

prompt.text: "I'm going to visit my grandmother tomorrow." — She said that she ___ going to visit ___ grandmother the day after.

items.hintDe: am wird zu was. Und "my" (aus ihrer Sicht) wird aus deiner Perspektive zu "her".
items.explainDe: am → was, und das Pronomen passt sich an: aus my (sie selbst) wird her. Also: She said that she was going to visit her grandmother the day after.

prompt.text: "I have already eaten lunch." — He told me that he ___ already ___ lunch.

items.hintDe: Das present perfect (have eaten) geht eine Stufe zurück: have wird zu had, eaten bleibt die 3. Form.
items.explainDe: have + 3. Form wird beim Wiedergeben zu had + 3. Form. Also: He told me that he had already eaten lunch.

prompt.text: "I have never been to London." — She said that she ___ never ___ to London.

items.hintDe: have wird zu had, been (3. Form) bleibt gleich.
items.explainDe: have been wird beim Wiedergeben zu had been. Also: She said that she had never been to London.

prompt.text: "I bought this book here last week." — She said that she had bought ___ book ___ the week before.

items.hintDe: Beim Wiedergeben wird "this" zu "that" und "here" zu "there".
items.explainDe: Ortsangaben verschieben sich: this → that, here → there. Also: ... she had bought that book there the week before.

prompt.text: "We were waiting for the train yesterday." — They said that they ___ been waiting for the train the day ___.

items.hintDe: were waiting geht eine Stufe zurück zu had been waiting. Und yesterday wird zu "the day before".
items.explainDe: were + -ing wird zu had been + -ing; yesterday → the day before. Also: ... they had been waiting for the train the day before.

prompt.text: (said / told?) He ___ me that he was at the airport. — She ___ that she was busy.

items.hintDe: Mit Person (me) nimmst du told. Ohne Person nimmst du said.
items.explainDe: told braucht eine Person danach (told me), said steht ohne Person (said that ...).

prompt.text: "We have lost power in both engines." — Sully ___ Harten that they ___ ___ power in both engines.

items.hintDe: Vor der Person Harten brauchst du told. have lost geht eine Stufe zurück zu had lost.
items.explainDe: Mit Person → told. have + 3. Form wird zu had + 3. Form: had lost. (So steht es auch in der Story.)

prompt.text: (said / told?) She ___ me that the museum was closed.

items.hintDe: Direkt vor der Person (me) steht told, nicht said.
items.explainDe: told braucht eine Person danach (told me). said me ist falsch.

prompt.text: "I can't come." — He said that he ___ come.

items.hintDe: can't verschiebt sich eine Zeit zurück zu couldn't.
items.explainDe: Aus can't wird couldn't. Also: He said that he couldn't come.

prompt.text: "I will be there before nine." — Olivia said that she ___ be there before nine.

items.hintDe: will verschiebt sich eine Zeit zurück zu would.
items.explainDe: Aus will wird would. Also: Olivia said that she would be there before nine.

prompt.text: "I gave the keys to the teacher." — Ron said that he ___ given the keys to the teacher.

items.hintDe: Das past simple (gave) geht eine Stufe zurück zur Form had + 3. Form (had given).
items.explainDe: gave (2. Form) → had given (had + 3. Form). Also: Ron said that he had given the keys to the teacher.

prompt.text: "I want an ice cream." — He said that he ___ an ice cream.

items.hintDe: want geht eine Zeit zurück zur 2. Form wanted.
items.explainDe: Nach said wird want zu wanted. Also: He said that he wanted an ice cream.

prompt.text: "I am going there this week." — He said that he was going there ___ week.

items.hintDe: Beim Wiedergeben wird "this week" zu "that week".
items.explainDe: this (Zeit) → that. Also: He said that he was going there that week.

prompt.text: (said / told?) Mary ___ us that she was going to New York.

items.hintDe: Direkt vor der Person (us) steht told.
items.explainDe: Mit Person danach (us) → told. Also: Mary told us that she was going to New York.

prompt.text: (said / told?) Peter ___ that he would be busy.

items.hintDe: Hier steht keine Person nach dem Verb, also nimmst du said.
items.explainDe: Ohne Person danach → said. Also: Peter said that he would be busy.

prompt.text: Sortiere: Ist die Zeit richtig nach hinten verschoben?

members: He said that he was tired.
She said that she lived there.
They said that they could come.
He said that he is tired.
She said that she lives there.
They said that they can come.

items.hintDe: Nach said muss die Zeit eine Stufe zurück: is → was, live → lived, can → could.
items.explainDe: Richtig sind die Sätze mit verschobener Zeit (was, lived, could). Falsch sind die mit Gegenwart (is, lives, can).

prompt.text: Sortiere: Ist das Wort für "sagen" (said/told) richtig verwendet?

members: He said that he was busy.
She told me that she was busy.
They said that they were tired.
He said me that he was busy.
She told that she was busy.
They said me that they were tired.

items.hintDe: said steht ohne Person, told steht mit Person. "said me" und "told that" (ohne Person) sind falsch.
items.explainDe: Richtig: said that ... / told me that ... Falsch: said me ... (said braucht keine Person) und told that ... (told braucht eine Person).

prompt.text: Sortiere die Sätze nach dem passenden Wort für "sagen" (said/told).

groups.label: said
members: He said that he was tired.
She said that she lived there.
They said that they were busy.

groups.label: told
members: He told me that he was tired.
She told us that she lived there.
They told me that they were busy.

items.hintDe: told hat immer eine Person danach (told me/us). said hat keine Person danach.
items.explainDe: Sätze ohne Person nach dem Verb gehören zu said. Sätze mit Person (me/us) gehören zu told.

prompt.text: "I'm tired." — He said that he ___ tired.

items.hintDe: Eine einzelne Person (he) und eine Zeit zurück: aus is wird was.
items.explainDe: Nach said geht is zurück zu was. were passt nur zu we/you/they.

prompt.text: "I can drive a car." — She said that she ___ drive a car.

items.hintDe: can verschiebt sich eine Zeit zurück.
items.explainDe: Aus can wird could. would gehört zu will, nicht zu can.

prompt.text: "I will help you." — She told me that she ___ help me.

items.hintDe: will verschiebt sich eine Zeit zurück.
items.explainDe: Aus will wird would. Also: She told me that she would help me.

prompt.text: "I am studying." — She said that she ___ studying.

items.hintDe: Bei she verschiebt sich am/is zu was.
items.explainDe: am/is + -ing wird zu was + -ing. Also: She said that she was studying.

prompt.text: "I am leaving now." — She said that she ___ leaving ___.

items.hintDe: am wird zu was, und now wird zu then.
items.explainDe: am → was, now → then. Also: She said that she was leaving then.

prompt.text: "I am reading this book here." — He said that he was reading ___ book ___.

items.hintDe: this wird zu that, here wird zu there.
items.explainDe: this → that, here → there. Also: ... he was reading that book there.

prompt.text: "We have visited New York." — They said that they ___ visited New York.

items.hintDe: have + 3. Form geht eine Stufe zurück: have wird zu had.
items.explainDe: have visited → had visited. Also: They said that they had visited New York.

prompt.text: "I live here." — He said that he lived ___.

items.hintDe: Die Ortsangabe here wird beim Wiedergeben zu there.
items.explainDe: here → there. Also: He said that he lived there.

prompt.text: "I must go now." — He said that he ___ go then.

items.hintDe: must verschiebt sich beim Wiedergeben zu had to.
items.explainDe: Aus must wird had to. Also: He said that he had to go then.

prompt.text: Ordne jeder Gegenwartsform die Form zu, die sie beim Wiedergeben bekommt.

items.hintDe: Jedes Wort geht eine Zeit zurück: like → liked, can → could, will → would, must → had to, is → was.
items.explainDe: Verschiebe jede Form eine Stufe zurück: like → liked, want → wanted, can → could, will → would, must → had to, is → was.

prompt.text: Ordne jedem Wort die Form zu, die es beim Wiedergeben bekommt.

items.hintDe: Achte auf das verschobene Verb: like → liked, will → would, can → could, is → was.
items.explainDe: Jede Form verschiebt sich eine Zeit zurück: like → liked, will → would, can → could, is → was.

prompt.text: Ordne jedem Zeit- oder Ortswort die Form zu, die es beim Wiedergeben bekommt.

items.hintDe: Beim Wiedergeben verschieben sich Zeit- und Ortswörter: today → that day, yesterday → the day before, here → there.
items.explainDe: today → that day, yesterday → the day before, tomorrow → the day after, last week → the week before, here → there.

prompt.text: Gib den ganzen Satz wieder. Beginne mit: He told me ... — "I have lost my book."

answers.text: He told me that he had lost his book.
He told me he had lost his book.

items.hintDe: have lost → had lost, und my → his. Mit Person → told me.
items.explainDe: have lost → had lost; my → his. Mit Person: He told me that he had lost his book.

prompt.text: Gib den ganzen Satz wieder. Beginne mit: Sully told Harten ... — "We will land on the Hudson."

answers.text: Sully told Harten that they would land on the Hudson.
Sully told Harten they would land on the Hudson.

items.hintDe: will → would, we → they. Vor der Person Harten steht told.
items.explainDe: will → would; we → they. So steht es in der Story: Sully told Harten that they would land on the Hudson.

prompt.text: said / he / liked / that / she / chocolate

answers.text: He said that she liked chocolate.

items.hintDe: Beginne mit der Person, dann said that ..., dann der Nebensatz mit dem Verb in der 2. Form.
items.explainDe: Reihenfolge: He said that she liked chocolate. Nach said folgt that + Nebensatz.

prompt.text: the / teacher / said / that / had / we / to / study / more

answers.text: The teacher said that we had to study more.
The teacher said we had to study more.

items.hintDe: Ohne Person nach said. must wurde zu had to. Reihenfolge: ... said that we had to study more.
items.explainDe: The teacher said that we had to study more. "had to" ist die verschobene Form von must.

prompt.text: told / me / would / she / help / that / she / me

answers.text: She told me that she would help me.
She told me she would help me.

items.hintDe: Mit Person: She told me that ... Danach das verschobene will = would.
items.explainDe: Reihenfolge: She told me that she would help me. told braucht die Person (me) direkt danach.

prompt.text: "I am tired." → Anna said that she ___ (be) tired.

items.hintDe: is/am verschiebt sich zu was.
items.explainDe: Aus am wird was. Also: Anna said that she was tired.

prompt.text: "Sarah is studying English at a new school." → Tom told me that Sarah ___ (study) English at a new school.

items.hintDe: is studying verschiebt sich zu was studying.
items.explainDe: is + -ing wird zu was + -ing. Also: ... that Sarah was studying English ...

prompt.text: "We can't come to the party." → They said that they ___ (can / not) come to the party.

items.hintDe: can't verschiebt sich zu couldn't.
items.explainDe: Aus can't wird couldn't. Also: They said that they couldn't come to the party.

prompt.text: "You must do your homework." → The teacher told us that we ___ (must) do our homework.

items.hintDe: must verschiebt sich zu had to. Und das Pronomen wechselt: "you" wird zu "we".
items.explainDe: must → had to, und das Personenwort passt sich an (you → we). Also: ... that we had to do our homework.

prompt.text: "We will land on the river." → Sully said that they ___ (will) land on the river.

items.hintDe: will verschiebt sich zu would.
items.explainDe: Aus will wird would. So steht es auch in der Story: Sully said that they would land on the river.

prompt.text: Gib den ganzen Satz wieder. Beginne mit: She said ... — "I live here."

answers.text: She said that she lived there.

items.hintDe: live → lived (eine Zeit zurück) und here → there.
items.explainDe: live wird zu lived, here wird zu there. Also: She said that she lived there.

prompt.text: Gib den ganzen Satz wieder. Beginne mit: He told me ... — "I saw a good film yesterday."

answers.text: He told me that he had seen a good film the day before.
He told me he had seen a good film the day before.

items.hintDe: saw geht eine Stufe zurück zu had seen, und yesterday wird zu "the day before".
items.explainDe: Past simple (saw) → had + 3. Form (had seen); yesterday → the day before. Mit Person: He told me ...

prompt.text: Gib den ganzen Satz wieder. Beginne mit: Lisa said ... — "I don't have any homework today."

answers.text: Lisa said that she didn't have any homework that day.
Lisa said she didn't have any homework that day.
Lisa said that she did not have any homework that day.
Lisa said she did not have any homework that day.

items.hintDe: don't have → didn't have, today → that day, und das Pronomen I → she.
items.explainDe: don't have wird zu didn't have, today wird zu that day, I wird zu she. Also: Lisa said that she didn't have any homework that day.

prompt.text: Gib den ganzen Satz wieder. Beginne mit: Sully told Harten ... — "We can't make runway 1."

answers.text: Sully told Harten that they couldn't make runway 1.
Sully told Harten they couldn't make runway 1.
Sully told Harten that they could not make runway 1.
Sully told Harten they could not make runway 1.

items.hintDe: can't → couldn't, und we → they. Vor der Person Harten steht told.
items.explainDe: can't → couldn't, we → they. So steht es in der Story: Sully told Harten that they couldn't make runway 1.

prompt.text: Gib den ganzen Satz wieder. Beginne mit: Skiles told Sully ... — "You have done something special."

answers.text: Skiles told Sully that he had done something special.
Skiles told Sully he had done something special.

items.hintDe: have done → had done, und you → he (Skiles spricht über Sully). Vor der Person Sully steht told.
items.explainDe: have done → had done; you → he. So steht es sinngemäß in der Story: Skiles told Sully that he had done something special.

prompt.text: Gib den ganzen Satz wieder. Beginne mit: Mum said ... — "You are going to miss the train."

answers.text: Mum said that I was going to miss the train.
Mum said I was going to miss the train.

items.hintDe: are wird zu was, und "you" (du) wird aus deiner Sicht zu "I".
items.explainDe: are → was, you → I (deine Mutter spricht über dich). Also: Mum said that I was going to miss the train.

prompt.text: Er sagte, dass er müde war.

answers.text: He said that he was tired.

items.hintDe: Ohne Person nach dem sagen-Verb: He said (that) ... Und müde war = was tired.
items.explainDe: "sagte, dass" ohne Person → He said that ... Das war wird zu was.

prompt.text: Er erzählte uns, dass er am Tag davor sein Buch verloren hatte.

answers.text: He told us that he had lost his book the day before.
He told us he had lost his book the day before.

items.hintDe: Mit Person (uns) → told us. "hatte verloren" = had lost. "am Tag davor" = the day before.
items.explainDe: erzählte uns → told us; hatte verloren → had lost; am Tag davor → the day before.

prompt.text: Er sagte mir, dass er die Hausübung gemacht hatte.

answers.text: He told me that he had done his homework.
He told me he had done his homework.

items.hintDe: Mit Person (mir) → told me. "gemacht hatte" = had done.
items.explainDe: sagte mir → told me; gemacht hatte → had done. Also: He told me that he had done his homework.

prompt.text: Sue sagte, dass sie Kopfschmerzen hatte.

answers.text: Sue said that she had a headache.
Sue said she had a headache.

items.hintDe: Kopfschmerzen haben = to have a headache. Ohne Person: She said (that) ...
items.explainDe: "hatte" → had. Ohne Person nach said: She said that she had a headache.

prompt.text: Mia erzählte mir, dass sie am Tag danach nach Wien fahren würde.

answers.text: Mia told me that she would go to Vienna the day after.
Mia told me she would go to Vienna the day after.
Mia told me that she would travel to Vienna the day after.

items.hintDe: Mit Person (mir) → told me. "würde fahren" = would go. "am Tag danach" = the day after.
items.explainDe: erzählte mir → told me; würde → would; am Tag danach → the day after. Also: She told me that she would go to Vienna the day after.
